top_signals.title: Temporary inflation slowdown set to boost Burnham
top_signals.summary: Inflation is expected to have eased last month in a welcome but temporary boost for Andy Burnham’s incoming government.  The headline rate of Consumer Prices Index (CPI) inflation is tipped to have eased from 2.8 per cent in May to 2.6 per cent in June, according to some economists.  The latest rate of inflation will [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: South Korean regulator begins sanctions process against Dunamu: Report
top_signals.summary: The proceedings come as South Korea’s Virtual Asset User Protection Act lacks explicit sanctions provisions for hacking and computer system incidents, leaving the scope of penalties uncertain.

top_signals.title: Thames Water creditors expect Burnham talks despite legal contigency plans
top_signals.summary: The creditors bidding to rescue Thames Water remain confident they will be able to present a revised takeover proposal to Andy Burnham’s government, despite preparing for the possibility ministers reject the deal, a source told City AM. People close to the London & Valley Water (L&VW) consortium said they expected to engage with the new [...]
